name: MIMIC-EU [Minimal]
description: Creates an Atlas of anonymized Acute Care cases to demonstrate the INDICATE platform's capability for aggregating ICU datasets and developing clinically relevant AI algorithms.
justification: This use case aims to demonstrate the capabilities of the INDICATE platform for the aggregation of ICU datasets for secondary analysis and the development of clinically relevant AI algorithms by establishing an Atlas of anonymized Acute Care cases which we named MIMIC-EU. The atlas incorporates multiparameter physiological signals, laboratory results, ICU procedures, medications, imaging reports, clinical notes, and outcomes including ICU/in-hospital mortality, organ failure, organ support, and length of stay. Development takes an iterative approach, starting with the onboarding of mature and leading data providers (UDUS, CHU-Rennes) and continuously onboarding more data providers thereafter. Key tasks include: (T6.1.1) Establishment of the formal MIMIC-EU workgroup collaborating with the Data Provider Support Office to define a minimal viable dataset and associated common data model and anonymisation requirements; (T6.1.2) Drafting of template data access agreements and terms of use for data providers with the aid of ELSI framework experts; (T6.1.3) Integration of the MIMIC-EU atlas with the centralised metadata catalogue as a predefined selection across the federated infrastructure; (T6.1.4) Creation of fully anonymous synthetic dataset enabling users to validate data requests before execution; (T6.1.5) Implementation of secure workspace supporting user-provided tools or predefined analytical applications; (T6.1.6) Deployment of security protocols and cybersecurity safeguards; (T6.1.7) Clinical demonstrations addressing hemodynamic monitoring, glucose metabolism, regional treatment variations, rare diseases, and geriatric/oncology care trajectories. Led by Christian Jung at Heinrich-Heine-Universität Düsseldorf.
